data.title: Nasta Pet Food acquires FirstMate Pet Foods, an independent pet food manufacturer in Canada, with Taplow Ventures; enhances its North American presence.
data.description: A French-based family-owned international group specializing in ultra-premium pet nutrition, Nasta Pet Food, has recently acquired FirstMate Pet Foods, an independent premium pet food manufacturer located in British Columbia, Canada. This acquisition has resulted in Nasta Pet Food becoming the sole owner of FirstMate and its parent company, Taplow Ventures Ltd. The integration of FirstMate into Nasta Pet Food's operations aims to enhance the Group's commercial and manufacturing capabilities.

data.title: Bitcoin drops to $66,000, altcoins struggle. 84,160 traders liquidated, losing $192.05M. Robinhood creates $1B fund for retail investors.
data.description: Bitcoin dropped to $66,000 due to renewed macro-driven selling, with major altcoins struggling to break crucial resistance levels. The current prices for cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin at $66,526.02, Ethereum at $1,959.58, Solana at $81.39, XRP at $1.44, Dogecoin at $0.09919, and Shiba Inu at $0.056341. Notable statistics reveal that 84,160 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses of $192.05 million in the last 24 hours. There were net outflows of $104.9 million from spot Bitcoin ETFs, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw net inflows of $48.6 million. Additionally, the top gainers in the past day were World Liberty Financial, Kite, and Cosmos. In other news, Robinhood has launched a $1 billion fund to provide retail investors with access to investments in Space X and Stripe. Furthermore, experts predict that 2027 could be a groundbreaking year for cryptocurrencies. For further details, the complete article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ATN to report Q4 and full year 2025 results on March 4, 2026, hosting conference call on March 5 at 10:00 a.m. ET.
data.description: ATN International, Inc, a top digital infrastructure and communication services provider, is set to unveil its results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on March 4, 2026, post-market close. The company, trading on Nasdaq under ATNI, plans to conduct a conference call on March 5, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. ET to delve into the details of its performance.

data.title: KBRA rates Benchmark 2026-B42, $729.2 million CMBS deal with 123 properties across 43 MSAs, New York leading with 33.9% balance.
data.description: In New York, KBRA has issued preliminary ratings for 15 classes of Benchmark 2026-B42, a CMBS conduit deal worth $729.2 million. These ratings are assigned to a pool of 62 commercial mortgage loans tied to 123 properties across 43 MSAs. The top three MSAs include New York (33.9% of pool balance), Phoenix (11.0%), and Washington - NoVA - MD (4.1%). The collateral properties encompass various major property types, with four specific types accounting for a significant portion of the pool.

data.title: Brandywine Realty Trust declares $0.08 quarterly dividend per common share, $0.32 annual rate; earnings release in Q1 2026.
data.description: On February 18, 2026, Brandywine Realty Trust (NYSE:BDN) revealed that it has authorized a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 for each common share and OP unit, with the payment scheduled for April 16, 2026, to shareholders of record by April 2, 2026. At an annual rate, this quarterly dividend amounts to $0.32 per common share.
